title: PRODUCT next_action task each turn
purpose: B4.1 defines product next action task each turn for the agent-driven expert system. Cognition & routing — [S0](B1.1-s0-deterministic-mandatory-first.md)–S4, conductor, workers, dual-stack.
scope: Owns `B4.1` only; siblings under `B4` must not duplicate this spec.
Aligns with minimal HITL: H1 plan, H2 blocker, H3 sign-off ([INTRO-1.2](INTRO-1.2-human-touchpoint-contract-h1-h2-h3.md)).
Conflicts resolve in favor of [Vision §4 — Branch B — Cognition & routing plane](../../full-automation-vision-and-hierarchy.md#4-branch-b-cognition-routing-plane).
behavior_steps: Each product turn executes exactly one `next_action` from state.json—one task card or one skill phase aligned with Plane C pipeline order—without skipping to push or refactor while implement evidence is missing.
[B1.1](B1.1-s0-deterministic-mandatory-first.md) sync-state and route-tier read state.json first when available; the conductor interprets the journal mirror only when S0 output is silent.
Under interleaved [B4.2](B4.2-platform-promotion-queue-peek-drain.md) scheduling, product turns still advance `next_action` atomically so Plane C progress does not desync from recorded state.
Workers execute the delegated slice of `next_action`; only the conductor advances past the task after verify passes or structured H2 is recorded per [G1.3](G1.3-last-verify-before-advance.md).
If journal and state.json disagree on `next_action`, or required evidence is missing, [A2.1](A2.1-preflight-check-pipeline-blocked-extended.md) blocks the turn until dual-write reconciles.

Edge case `B4.1` variant 3: verify state dual-write before continuing pursuit.
Edge case `B4.1` variant 4: verify state dual-write before continuing pursuit.
Pass 3: add regression test or evidence path specific to `B4.1`.
Pass 3: cross-link related nodes in same branch index.

**False complete:** Item marked done without artifact â†’ audit-hierarchy-depth.py re-enqueues deepen pass.
**Scope bleed:** Worker edits journal/state during planning-only expansion â†’ forbidden in vision-expansion-prompt.
**Stale design:** Upstream vision Â§ changes â†’ reconcile-stale adds deepen items for affected ids.
implementation: Map `B4.1` to v2.14â€“v2.23 release row in SEC-15-index.md.
Create or extend S0 script if behavior is file-derived.
Add unit test under tests/unit/test_b4_1.py when script exists.
Validate `B4.1` against SEC-15 release checklist and parent index links.
Document `B4.1` in parent index with verify command and release tag.
Add checklist row in SEC-15 release doc for `B4.1`.
acceptance: [ ] Named script, skill, or test path exists or is listed in SEC-15 release row
narrative: B4.1 defines product next action task each turn for the agent-driven expert system. Cognition & routing — [S0](B1.1-s0-deterministic-mandatory-first.md)–S4, conductor, workers, dual-stack. In practice, the system each product turn executes exactly one `next_action` from state.json—one task card or one skill phase aligned with plane c pipeline order—without skipping to push or refactor while implement evidence is missing, then [b1.1](b1.1-s0-deterministic-mandatory-first.md) sync-state and route-tier read state.json first when available; the conductor interprets the journal mirror only when s0 output is silent.
reader_narrative:
<!-- prose-source: agent plane-b 2026-06-28 -->

Each product turn executes the current `next_action` task from state.json—one task card, one skill phase—aligned with Plane C pipelines. The conductor does not skip ahead to push or refactor while implement evidence is missing.

Task selection is S0 where scripts exist; otherwise conductor reads state and journal mirror. This capability binds Plane B routing to Plane C execution order so interleaved product and platform scheduling does not desynchronize project progress from recorded next_action.
